title: bromus racemosus var. neglectus
intro: Bromus racemosus var. neglectus, also known as fescue grass or false brome, is a type of perennial grass native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its use as a diuretic and its ability to aid in the treatment of kidney and bladder issues. The health benefits of Bromus racemosus var. neglectus include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of urinary tract infections. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and rhizomes are often dried and used in teas or tinctures to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
